name: Louis Metcalf
abstract: Louis Metcalf (February 28, 1905 – October 27, 1981) was an American jazz trumpeter who played for a short time with Duke Ellington.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: There was a trumpet player with him whom I had met named Louis Metcalf.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: Metcalf was in the band then. Because we used to give him hell...

about_quotes.speaker: J. C. Higginbotham
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript shows the INTERVIEWER narrating a story about King Oliver and Louis Metcalf ('Louis Metcalf played the trumpet part... That was the first recording ever I made in my life'). The interviewee (J.C. Higginbotham) does not speak in the provided context, nor does the interviewer attribute

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Louis Metcalf was an old-timer who was a follower of Louis Armstrong. He went to Canada and became ensconced in the Canadian scene up there and was on the scene for a long, long time
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, it actually depended who was in town. Any of the bands that came in town, they were -- oh, Louis Metcalf. Louis Metcalf used to come in there.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: I loaned him Dewey Maycare Louis Metcalf... I loaned him, so I said, the better man that he should have was Louis Metcalf.
